title: `podman quadlet install <url>` should be able to install a whole repository directory
body:
### Feature request description

I found no way for podman quadlet to install a directory from a git repository. Here's a sample error message:

```
podman quadlet install https://codeberg.org/herzenschein/herz-quadlet/etherpad

Error: quadlet "https://codeberg.org/herzenschein/herz-quadlet/etherpad" failed to install: "" is not a supported Quadlet file type
Error: errors occurred installing some Quadlets
```

As it currently stands, `podman quadlet install <url>` can only install single files.

While I understand [git forges themselves don't have support for downloading directories](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7106012/download-a-single-folder-or-directory-from-a-github-repository), it would still be useful to have this functionality in some way.

### Suggest potential solution

Something like go-getter or even just outright doing a tmp shallow clone and copying the directory (I'd find this better as go-getter syntax doesn't look intuitive).

This would be way more useful than having to name specific files in the repository (which is limited only to supported file extensions).

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
